mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 8.0 percent signals accelerating labor market recovery and rising consumer income, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below the 8.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production signals immediate supply-side contraction and weakening corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO changes are followed by positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates combined with a consumer price index above 230.0 signal negative real yields and impending monetary policy normalization, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue fundamentals, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly nonfarm payroll increase exceeding 100 thousand persons confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by more than 100.0 thousand
failure_condition: Payroll gains above 100 thousand are followed by negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized demand and supply shock, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline condition yields positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.
